topic.what: benchmark for evaluating LLMs ability to determine whether algorithms satisfy differential privacy guarantees
topic.how: carefully curated instances asking binary yes/no decisions on DP correctness across textbook mechanisms and advanced research algorithms
topic.why_matters: bridges gap between LLM capabilities and expert-level DP reasoning needed for automated privacy-preserving system development

characteristics.evidence: GPT-5-High achieves near-perfect accuracy on Category 1 (Laplace, Gaussian, ExpoMech, RNM variants)
characteristics.context: 588 instances across 6 DP mechanisms using function bank with 49 functions

characteristics.evidence: GPT-5-High (F1=0.742) and Gemini-3-Pro (F1=0.748) achieve best performance on 132 Category 2 instances
characteristics.context: 132 instances from 16 research topics (DP-Adam, Quantile Estimation, Accounting easiest; Smooth Sensitivity, PATE hardest)
720 total instances spanning broad DP topics and difficulty levels

characteristics.what_it_measures: performance difference between best open vs closed models
characteristics.evidence: Best open-source (DeepSeek-V3.1-chat: 0.841) vs GPT-5-High (0.995) on Category 1 shows 15.4% gap
characteristics.context: Substantial gap remains for open-source models on textbook DP reasoning

characteristics.what_it_measures: error rate on challenging DP topics
characteristics.evidence: F1 scores 0.36-0.62 for most models on Category 2 indicate high failure rates on advanced topics
characteristics.context: No model achieves strong consistent performance on advanced algorithms; Smooth Sensitivity, PATE at ~33% accuracy

characteristics.evidence: Theorem augmentation yields largest gains (+0.2-0.3 F1) on 18 hardest Category 2 questions
characteristics.context: External knowledge integration (theorem injection, RAG) shows promising direction for improvement
